risk.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/sign-base64-encoded-message\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/evm/sign/message\",\"title\":\"Sign EIP-191 message via end user EVM account\",\"description\":\"Signs an [EIP-191](https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-191) message with the given end user EVM account.\\n\\nPer the specification, the message in the request body is prepended with `0x19 \u003c0x45 (E)\u003e \u003cthereum Signed Message:\\\\n\\\" + len(message)\u003e` before being signed.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/sign-eip-191-message-via-end-user-evm-account\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/evm/sign/typed-data\",\"title\":\"Sign EIP-712 typed data via end user EVM account\",\"description\":\"Signs account.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/sign-eip-712-typed-data-via-end-user-evm-account\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/evm/sign/transaction\",\"title\":\"Sign transaction via end user EVM account\",\"description\":\"Signs a transaction with the given end user EVM dynamic fee transaction](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-1559.md). The developer is responsible for ensuring that the unsigned transaction is valid, as the API will not validate the transaction.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/sign-transaction-via-end-user-evm-account\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/solana/sign/transaction\",\"title\":\"Sign transaction via end user Solana account\",\"description\":\"Signs a transaction with the given end user Solana account.\\nThe unsigned transaction should be serialized into a byte array and then encoded as base64.\\n**Transaction types**\\nThe following transaction types are supported:\\n* [Legacy transactions](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/classes/Transaction.html)\\n* [Versioned transactions](https://solana-labs.github.io/solana-web3.js/classes/VersionedTransaction.html)\\nThe developer is responsible for ensuring that the unsigned transaction is valid, as the API will not validate the transaction.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/sign-transaction-via-end-user-solana-account\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/solana/sign/x402-payment\",\"title\":\"Sign x402 payment via end user Solana account\",\"description\":\"Signs an x402 payment payload using the end user's given Solana account.\\nAccepts the full x402 payment required response body from a resource server plus an index into the accepts array selecting which payment option to sign. The paymentRequired envelope's x402Version, resource, and extensions are carried through into the signed payment payload; only the selected accept entry becomes paymentPayload.accepted.\\nReturns a signed payment payload that can be base64-encoded and sent in the PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header of the resource request.\\nIf acceptsIndex is out of range for paymentRequired.accepts, or the selected accept is not a Solana network payment option, the request fails with 422.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/sign-x402-payment-via-end-user-solana-account\"},{\"openapi\":\"delete /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/address/{address}/delegation\",\"title\":\"Revoke account-scoped delegation for end user\",\"description\":\"Revokes the active account-scoped delegation for the specified end user account. Other account-scoped delegations for the same user are unaffected. This operation can be performed by the end user themselves or by a developer using their API key.\\nWhen the address corresponds to an EVM Smart Account, this revokes the delegation for the Smart Account's owner EOA.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/end-user-accounts/revoke-account-scoped-delegation-for-end-user\"},{\"openapi\":\"delete /v2/embedded-wallet-api/end-users/{userId}/delegation\",\"title\":\"Revoke delegation for end user\",\"description\":\"Revokes all active delegations for the specified end user. Either `paymentAmount` or `purchaseAmount` must be provided.\\n\\nThis API currently only supports the payment method `GUEST_CHECKOUT_APPLE_PAY`.\\n\\nFor detailed integration instructions and to get access to this API, refer to the  [Apple Pay Onramp API docs](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/onramp-\u0026-offramp/onramp-apis/apple-pay-onramp-api).\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onramp/create-an-onramp-order\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/onramp/sessions\",\"title\":\"Create an onramp session\",\"description\":\"$56\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onramp/create-an-onramp-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/onramp/limits\",\"title\":\"Get onramp user limits\",\"description\":\"Returns the transaction limits for an onramp user based on their payment method and user identifier. Returns a `verificationId` that must be passed to the Submit Onramp Verification endpoint along with the OTP code within 10 minutes.\\n\\n**Access to this API requires allowlisting.** During Onramp Headless API onboarding, contact the Onramp team to enable Onramp-managed verification for your application.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onramp/initiate-onramp-verification\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/onramp/limits/upgrade\",\"title\":\"Request limit upgrade\",\"description\":\"Requests a limit upgrade for an onramp user. Only phone number is currently supported as a userId.\\n\\nThe default API mode submits identity information directly and returns HTTP 202 when the request is accepted. Embedded mode (`interactionMode: embedded`) returns a Coinbase-hosted `upgradeUrl` where the user enters identity information.\\n\\nThe verification process is asynchronous. After calling this endpoint, use the [Get Onramp User Limits](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onramp/get-onramp-user-limits) endpoint to check the status in the `limitUpgradeOptions` array.\\n\\n**Prerequisites:**\\n- The phone number must have been previously verified by your app via OTP. - Upgrades may not be available until a certain number of successful transactions by the user.\\n\\n**Supported fields:**\\n- `ssnLast4`: Last 4 digits of the Social Security Number (no dashes or spaces).\\n- `dateOfBirth`: Date of birth (day, month, year as zero-padded strings).\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onramp/request-limit-upgrade\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/onramp/verifications/{verificationId}/submit\",\"title\":\"Submit onramp verification\",\"description\":\"Submits the OTP code to complete verification. On success, marks the verification as verified and returns the same `verificationId`. The destination does not need to be re-sent. Onramp uses the value captured at initiation time.\\n\\nThe returned `verificationId` should be stored on the user's device and passed to the Create Onramp Order endpoint. It is valid for 60 days.\\n\\n**Access to this API requires allowlisting.** During Onramp Headless API onboarding, contact the Onramp team to enable Onramp-managed verification for your application.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onramp/submit-onramp-verification\"},{\"group\":\"Webhooks\",\"pages\":[{\"openapi\":\"webhook offrampTransactionCreated\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Offramp transaction created\",\"title\":\"Offramp transaction created\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `offramp.transaction.created` webhook event is emitted. The response is paginated, and by default, returns 20 balances per page.\\n**Note:** This endpoint is still under development and does not yet provide strong freshness guarantees. Specifically, balances of new tokens can, on occasion, take up to ~30 seconds to appear, while balances of tokens already belonging to an address will generally be close to chain tip. Freshness of new token balances will improve over the coming weeks.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/evm-token-balances/list-evm-token-balances\"}]},{\"group\":\"Faucets\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Faucets\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Overview\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/faucets/faucets\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/evm/faucet\",\"title\":\"Request funds on EVM test networks\",\"description\":\"Request funds from the CDP Faucet on supported EVM test networks.\\n\\nFaucets are available for ETH, USDC, EURC, and cbBTC on Base Sepolia and Ethereum Sepolia, and for ETH only on Ethereum Hoodi.\\n\\nTo prevent abuse, we enforce rate limits within a rolling 24-hour window to control the amount of funds that can be requested.\\nThese limits are applied at both the CDP User level and the blockchain address level.\\nA single blockchain address cannot exceed the specified limits, even if multiple users submit requests to the same address.\\n\\n| Token | Amount per Faucet Request |Rolling 24-hour window Rate Limits|\\n|:-----:|:-------------------------:|:--------------------------------:|\\n| ETH   | 0.0001 ETH                | 0.1 ETH                          |\\n| USDC  | 1 USDC                    | 10 USDC                          |\\n| EURC  | 1 EURC                    | 10 EURC                          |\\n| cbBTC | 0.0001 cbBTC              | 0.001 cbBTC                      |\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/faucets/request-funds-on-evm-test-networks\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/solana/faucet\",\"title\":\"Request funds on Solana devnet\",\"description\":\"Request funds from the CDP Faucet on Solana devnet.\\n\\nFaucets are available for SOL, USDC, and CBTUSD.\\n\\nTo prevent abuse, we enforce rate limits within a rolling 24-hour window to control the amount of funds that can be requested.\\nThese limits are applied at both the CDP Project level and the blockchain address level.\\nA single blockchain address cannot exceed the specified limits, even if multiple users submit requests to the same address.\\n\\n| Token  | Amount per Faucet Request |Rolling 24-hour window Rate Limits|\\n|:-----: |:-------------------------:|:--------------------------------:|\\n| SOL    | 0.00125 SOL               | 0.0125 SOL                       |\\n| USDC   | 1 USDC                    | 10 USDC                          |\\n| CBTUSD | 1 CBTUSD                  | 10 CBTUSD                        |\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/faucets/request-funds-on-solana-devnet\"}]},{\"group\":\"Onchain Data\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Onchain The response is paginated, and by default, returns 20 balances per page.\\n\\n**Note:** This endpoint provides \u003c1 second freshness from chain tip, \u003c500ms response latency for wallets with reasonable token history, and 99.9% uptime for production use.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onchain-data/list-evm-token-balances\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/data/evm/token-ownership/{network}/{address}\",\"title\":\"List token addresses for account\",\"description\":\"Retrieve all ERC-20 token contract addresses that an account has ever received tokens from.\\nAnalyzes transaction history to discover token interactions.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/onchain-data/list-token-addresses-for-account\"},{\"group\":\"Webhooks\",\"pages\":[{\"openapi\":\"webhook onchainActivityDetected\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Onchain activity detected\",\"title\":\"Onchain activity detected\",\"description\":\"Triggered when onchain activity matching your subscription filters is detected. Use `labels` on your webhook subscription to filter which onchain events are delivered (see the Create Webhook Subscription endpoint for allowed labels).\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/webhooks/webhook-onchain-activity-detected\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook walletActivityDetected\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Wallet activity detected\",\"title\":\"Wallet activity detected\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `wallet.activity.detected` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/webhooks/webhook-wallet-activity-detected\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook walletActivityMulti\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Wallet activity multi\",\"title\":\"Wallet activity multi\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `wallet.activity.multi` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/webhooks/webhook-wallet-activity-multi\"}]}]},{\"group\":\"Solana Token Balances\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Solana Token Balances\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Overview\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/solana-token-balances/solana-token-balances\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/solana/token-balances/{network}/{address}\",\"title\":\"List Solana token balances\",\"description\":\"Lists the token balances of a Solana address on a given network. The balances include SPL tokens and the native SOL token. The response is paginated, and by default, returns 20 balances per page.\\n\\n**Note:** This endpoint is still under development and does not yet provide strong availability or freshness guarantees. Freshness and availability of new token balances will improve over the coming weeks.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/solana-token-balances/list-solana-token-balances\"}]},{\"group\":\"SQL API\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"SQL API\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Overview\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/sql-api/sql-api\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/data/query/schema\",\"title\":\"Get schema details\",\"description\":\"Retrieve the schema information for the available tables in the SQL API's indexed data.\\n\\nThis includes table names, column definitions, data types, and indexed fields.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/sql-api/get-schema-details\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/data/query/grammar\",\"title\":\"Get SQL grammar\",\"description\":\"Retrieve the SQL grammar for the SQL API.\\n\\nThe SQL queries that are supported by the SQL API are defined in ANTLR4 grammar which is evaluated by server before executing the query. This ensures the safety and soundness of the SQL query before execution.\\n\\nThis endpoint returns the ANTLR4 grammar that is used to evaluate the SQL queries so that developers can understand the SQL API and build SQL queries with high confidence and correctness.\\n\\nLLMs interact well with ANTLR4 grammar. You can feed the grammar directly into the LLMs to help generate SQL queries.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/sql-api/get-sql-grammar\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/data/query/run\",\"title\":\"Run SQL Query\",\"description\":\"$57\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/sql-api/run-sql-query\"}]}]},{\"group\":\"Customers\",\"tag\":\"Beta\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Customers\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Overview\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/customers/customers\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/customers/{customerId}\",\"title\":\"Get a customer\",\"description\":\"Get a customer by their ID. Returns the full customer object.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/customers/get-a-customer\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/customers\",\"title\":\"List customers\",\"description\":\"List all customers. The API will return all customers that the API Key has\\npermissions to access. Results are not returned in any guaranteed order.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/customers/list-customers\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/customers\",\"title\":\"Create a customer\",\"description\":\"Create a new customer record. You can optionally request capabilities\\nand submit verification requirements in the same request.\\n\\nThis endpoint supports creating a customer with:\\n- Minimal data (customer type only)\\n- Capability requests\\n- KYC data submission\\n- Combined request for capability and required info for verification\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/customers/create-a-customer\"},{\"openapi\":\"put /v2/customers/{customerId}\",\"title\":\"Update a customer\",\"description\":\"Update customer information and/or request capability changes.\\nThis endpoint supports partial updates. Provide only the fields you wish to change.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/customers/update-a-customer\"},{\"openapi\":\"delete /v2/customers/{customerId}\",\"title\":\"Delete a customer\",\"description\":\"Initiates deletion of the customer and all associated data. This operation\\nis idempotent — repeated calls for an already-deleted customer return 204.\\nA 404 is returned only when no customer with the given ID exists for the\\ncaller's entity.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/customers/delete-a-customer\"},{\"group\":\"Webhooks\",\"pages\":[{\"openapi\":\"webhook customersCustomerDeleted\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Customer deleted\",\"title\":\"Customer deleted\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `customers.customer.deleted` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/webhooks/webhook-customers-customer-deleted\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook customersCapabilityChanged\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Customers capability changed\",\"title\":\"Customers capability changed\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `customers.capability.changed` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/webhooks/webhook-customers-capability-changed\"}]}]},{\"group\":\"Webhooks\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Webhooks\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Overview\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/webhooks\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/data/webhooks/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\",\"title\":\"Get webhook subscription\",\"description\":\"Retrieve detailed information about a specific webhook subscription including\\nconfiguration, status, creation timestamp, and webhook signature secret.\\n\\n### Response Includes\\n- Subscription configuration and filters\\n- Target URL and custom headers\\n- Webhook signature secret for verification\\n- Creation timestamp and status\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/get-webhook-subscription\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/data/webhooks/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/events\",\"title\":\"List webhook subscription events\",\"description\":\"Retrieve webhook event delivery attempts for a specific subscription.\\nReturns event deliveries in descending order by creation time (newest first),\\nincluding delivery status, retry count, and response details.\\n\\n### Use Cases\\n- Debug webhook delivery failures and inspect response codes\\n- Monitor delivery status and retry counts\\n- Audit event delivery history for a subscription\\n- Verify that expected events were sent to webhook URLs\\n\\n### Filtering\\nUse optional query parameters to narrow results:\\n- `eventId` — find a specific event by ID\\n- `minCreatedAt` / `maxCreatedAt` — filter by time range\\n- `eventTypeNames` — filter by event type (comma-separated)\\n\\n**Note:** Results are limited to the 50 most recent events (newest first). No pagination is supported.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/list-webhook-subscription-events\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/data/webhooks/subscriptions\",\"title\":\"List webhook subscriptions\",\"description\":\"Retrieve a paginated list of webhook subscriptions for the authenticated project.\\nReturns subscriptions for all CDP product events (onchain, onramp/offramp, wallet, etc.)\\nin descending order by creation time.\\n\\n### Use Cases\\n- Monitor all active webhook subscriptions across CDP products\\n- Audit webhook configurations\\n- Manage subscription lifecycle\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/list-webhook-subscriptions\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/data/webhooks/subscriptions\",\"title\":\"Create webhook subscription\",\"description\":\"$58\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/create-webhook-subscription\"},{\"openapi\":\"put /v2/data/webhooks/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\",\"title\":\"Update webhook subscription\",\"description\":\"Update an existing webhook subscription's configuration including\\nevent types, target URL, filtering criteria, and enabled status.\\nAll required fields must be provided, even if they are not being changed.\\n\\n### Common Updates\\n- Change target URL or headers\\n- Add/remove event type filters\\n- Update multi-label filtering criteria\\n- Enable/disable subscription\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/update-webhook-subscription\"},{\"openapi\":\"delete /v2/data/webhooks/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\",\"title\":\"Delete webhook subscription\",\"description\":\"Permanently delete a webhook subscription and stop all event deliveries.\\nThis action cannot be undone.\\n\\n### Important Notes\\n- All webhook deliveries will cease immediately\\n- Subscription cannot be recovered after deletion\\n- Consider disabling instead of deleting for temporary pauses\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/v2/rest-api/webhooks/delete-webhook-subscription\"}]}]}]},{\"dropdown\":\"CDP Payment Acceptance Checkout\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/resource-guides/checkout\"}]},{\"group\":\"REST API\",\"pages\":[{\"group\":\"Payment Acceptance\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Payment Acceptance\",\"sidebarTitle\":\"Overview\",\"description\":null,\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/payment-acceptance-under-development\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/disbursements/{disbursementId}\",\"title\":\"Get a disbursement\",\"description\":\"Retrieves a single disbursement by its ID, including source, target, status, amount, associated onchain transactions, error details (if failed), and timestamps. Only returns the disbursement if the API key has permission to access it.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-a-disbursement\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}\",\"title\":\"Get a payment session\",\"description\":\"Retrieves a single payment session by its ID, including its current status, balances, and associated metadata. The API key must have permission to access the requested session.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-a-payment-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/authorizations/{authorizationId}\",\"title\":\"Get a payment session authorization\",\"description\":\"Retrieves a single authorization by its ID, including its current status, amount, and any associated onchain transactions.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-a-payment-session-authorization\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/captures/{captureId}\",\"title\":\"Get a payment session capture\",\"description\":\"Retrieves a single capture by its ID, including status, captured amount, associated onchain transactions, and timestamps. Only returns the capture if the API key has permission to access it.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-a-payment-session-capture\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/refunds/{refundId}\",\"title\":\"Get a payment session refund\",\"description\":\"Retrieves a single refund by its ID, including status, refunded amount, reason, associated onchain transactions, and timestamps. Only returns the refund if the API key has permission to access it.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-a-payment-session-refund\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/voids/{voidId}\",\"title\":\"Get a payment session void\",\"description\":\"Retrieves a single void by its ID, including status, voided amount, associated onchain transactions, and timestamps. Only returns the void if the API key has permission to access it.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-a-payment-session-void\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/authorizations/wallet/options\",\"title\":\"Get wallet authorization options\",\"description\":\"Returns the available wallet authorization options for a payment session. The session must be in `created` status.\\n\\nProvide one or more payer wallet addresses as query parameters. Each option specifies the currency, amount, network, and payloads the payer must sign to authorize the payment. Present the options to the payer and let them choose one, then call **Authorize Wallet** with the selected option.\\n\\nThis is a stateless read operation — the session is not modified.\\n\\nIf a requested address has no eligible authorization options (e.g. insufficient funds), it appears in `ineligibleAddresses` with a `code` and `message` instead of being absent from `options`. When an address is ineligible because of `insufficient_funds`, its `fundsRequired` array lists, per enabled (network, asset) combination, the current balance and required balance the payer needs to become eligible.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/get-wallet-authorization-options\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/coinbase-accounts/balances\",\"title\":\"List Coinbase account balances\",\"description\":\"Returns the balances held in the Coinbase account.\\n\\nThe `available` amount is the immediately spendable balance. The `total` amount also includes funds currently on hold. Both amounts are returned as decimal strings with 2 decimal places (e.g. `\\\"50.00\\\"`).\\n\\n**Authentication:** Requires a Coinbase OAuth Bearer token with the `coinbase:stablecoins:balance-read` scope.\\n\\n**Returned assets:** Currently always returns a single USDC entry. If the payer holds no USDC, the entry is still returned with an `available` and `total` of `\\\"0\\\"` so callers do not need to special-case a missing asset. Additional assets may be returned in the future without a contract change.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-coinbase-account-balances\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/disbursements\",\"title\":\"List disbursements\",\"description\":\"Returns a paginated list of disbursements created by the merchant. Each disbursement includes its source, target, status, amount, and timestamps. Only disbursements accessible by the current API key are returned.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-disbursements\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/authorizations\",\"title\":\"List payment session authorizations\",\"description\":\"Returns a paginated list of authorizations for a payment session. Each authorization represents a hold on funds and includes its current status and amount.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-payment-session-authorizations\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/captures\",\"title\":\"List payment session captures\",\"description\":\"Returns a paginated list of captures for a payment session. Each capture includes its status, amount, associated onchain transactions, and timestamps. Only captures accessible by the current API key are returned.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-payment-session-captures\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/refunds\",\"title\":\"List payment session refunds\",\"description\":\"Returns a paginated list of refunds for a payment session. Each refund includes its status, amount, reason, associated onchain transactions, and timestamps. Only refunds accessible by the current API key are returned.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-payment-session-refunds\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/voids\",\"title\":\"List payment session voids\",\"description\":\"Returns a paginated list of voids for a payment session. Each void includes its status, amount, associated onchain transactions, and timestamps. Only voids accessible by the current API key are returned.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-payment-session-voids\"},{\"openapi\":\"get /v2/payment-sessions\",\"title\":\"List payment sessions\",\"description\":\"Returns a paginated list of payment sessions that the API key has permission to access.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/list-payment-sessions\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/authorizations/coinbase\",\"title\":\"Authorize a payment session with a Coinbase account\",\"description\":\"Authorizes a payment session using the payer's Coinbase account authenticated via OAuth. The session must be in `created` status.\\n\\n**Authentication:** Requires a Coinbase OAuth Bearer token with the `coinbase:stablecoins:payment-create` scope.\\n\\nOn authorization, a hold is placed on the payer's funds. The authorization is returned in `pending` status and transitions asynchronously to `succeeded` or `failed`.\\n\\nIf `autoCapture` is enabled on the session, a capture is automatically created after a successful authorization.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/authorize-a-payment-session-with-a-coinbase-account\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/authorizations/wallet\",\"title\":\"Authorize a payment session with a wallet\",\"description\":\"Authorizes a payment session using the payer's wallet. The session must be in `created` status.\\n\\nThe `optionId` must match one of the options returned by the **Get Wallet Authorization Options** endpoint. Include the signed payloads for the selected option.\\n\\nOn authorization, a hold is placed on the payer's funds. The authorization is returned in `pending` status and transitions asynchronously to `succeeded` or `failed`.\\n\\nIf `autoCapture` is enabled on the session, a capture is automatically created after a successful authorization.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/authorize-a-payment-session-with-a-wallet\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/authorizations/x402\",\"title\":\"Authorize a payment session with x402\",\"description\":\"Authorizes a payment session using x402. The session must be in `created` status.\\n\\nThe client sends no request body. You may supply the base64-encoded x402-compliant payment payload in the optional **`PAYMENT-SIGNATURE`** header.\\n\\nOn authorization, a hold is placed on the payer's funds. The authorization is returned in `pending` status and transitions asynchronously to `succeeded` or `failed`.\\n\\n**402 Payment Required** may be returned when payment must be supplied before authorization can proceed. The **402** response uses the standard CDP **`Error`** JSON body and may include a **`PAYMENT-REQUIRED`** header (see the **402** response) describing accepted networks, assets, and amounts.\\n\\nIf `autoCapture` is enabled on the session, a capture is automatically created after a successful authorization.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/authorize-a-payment-session-with-x402\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/cancel\",\"title\":\"Cancel a payment session\",\"description\":\"Cancels a payment session before any funds have been authorized or captured. The session must be in `created` status. Cancel is blocked while any authorization action is pending.\\n\\nOnce canceled, no further actions can be performed on the session. This is the only way to terminate a pre-authorization session — expiry deadlines block actions but do not automatically transition the session to a terminal state.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/cancel-a-payment-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/captures\",\"title\":\"Capture a payment session\",\"description\":\"Captures authorized funds. The session must have a positive `capturable` balance and the `captureExpiresAt` deadline must not have passed.\\n\\nThis is an asynchronous operation. The capture is returned in `pending` status and transitions to `succeeded` or `failed`.\\n\\nMultiple partial captures are allowed. If `amount` is omitted, the full remaining capturable amount is captured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/capture-a-payment-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/disbursements\",\"title\":\"Create a disbursement\",\"description\":\"Creates a merchant-initiated payment of funds from a CDP account owned by the merchant to a Coinbase user account or onchain address. Used for standalone refunds, goodwill disbursements, rebates, and other merchant-driven payouts that are not tied to a specific payment session.\\n\\nThis is an asynchronous operation. The disbursement is returned in `pending` status and transitions to `succeeded` (with associated `onchainTransactions`) or `failed` (with `error`).\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/create-a-disbursement\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions\",\"title\":\"Create a payment session\",\"description\":\"Creates a payment session that defines what is being paid — amount, asset, and target. Optionally configure execution behavior, expiry deadlines, redirect URLs, and metadata.\\n\\nIf expiry deadlines are omitted, sensible defaults are applied (1 day for authorization, 7 days for capture, 30 days for refund). Expiries must be in ascending order — see the `expiries` object for details and constraints.\\n\\nReturns the session in `created` status. Next step: call one of the authorization endpoints (wallet or Coinbase) to authorize the payment.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/create-a-payment-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/refunds\",\"title\":\"Refund a payment session\",\"description\":\"Returns captured funds to the payer. The session must have a positive `refundable` balance and the `refundExpiresAt` deadline must not have passed.\\n\\nThis is an asynchronous operation. The refund is returned in `pending` status and transitions to `succeeded` or `failed`.\\n\\nIf `amount` is omitted, the full remaining refundable amount is refunded. Multiple partial refunds are supported.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/refund-a-payment-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"post /v2/payment-sessions/{paymentSessionId}/voids\",\"title\":\"Void a payment session\",\"description\":\"Releases all remaining capturable funds back to the payer. The session must have a positive `capturable` balance.\\n\\nThis is an asynchronous operation. The void is returned in `pending` status and transitions to `succeeded` or `failed`.\\n\\nAfter voiding, no further captures can be made. Unlike **Cancel**, which works before authorization, void works after authorization.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/void-a-payment-session\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptanceDisbursementFailed\",\"title\":\"Disbursement failed\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.disbursement.failed` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/disbursement-failed\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptanceDisbursementPending\",\"title\":\"Disbursement pending\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.disbursement.pending` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/disbursement-pending\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptanceDisbursementSucceeded\",\"title\":\"Disbursement succeeded\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.disbursement.succeeded` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/disbursement-succeeded\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptancePaymentSessionAuthorizationFailed\",\"title\":\"Payment session authorization failed\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.payment_session.authorization_failed` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/payment-session-authorization-failed\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptancePaymentSessionAuthorizationPending\",\"title\":\"Payment session authorization pending\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.payment_session.authorization_pending` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/payment-session-authorization-pending\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptancePaymentSessionAuthorizationSucceeded\",\"title\":\"Payment session authorization succeeded\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.payment_session.authorization_succeeded` webhook event is emitted. Your API will receive a POST request at the webhook URL you configured.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/cdp-payment-acceptance/rest-api/payment-acceptance-under-development/payment-session-created\"},{\"openapi\":\"webhook acceptancePaymentSessionRefundFailed\",\"title\":\"Payment session refund failed\",\"description\":\"Triggered when the `acceptance.payment_session.refund_failed` webhook event is emitted. dynamic list of staking transactions based on the specified network, asset, address, and [custom options](/staking/staking-api/introduction/api-usage#staking-options).\\n\\nIt returns a StakingOperation resource, a unified interface for the entire staking process. Regardless of network complexity, the API abstracts away all the underlying details through it.\\nYou can iterate through this resource to access each staking transaction and have them be signed and broadcasted to land the staking transaction onchain.\\n\\nRead up more about the stake operation and how to interact with it [here](/staking/staking-api/introduction/the-staking-operation).\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/build-a-new-staking-operation\"},{\"title\":\"Fetch historical staking balances\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/networks/{network_id}/addresses/{address_id}/stake/balances\",\"description\":\"Fetch historical staking balances for given address. This API is available for mainnet only.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/fetch-historical-staking-balances\"},{\"title\":\"Fetch staking rewards\",\"openapi\":\"POST /v1/stake/rewards/search\",\"description\":\"Fetch staking rewards for a list of addresses. This API is available for mainnet only.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/fetch-staking-rewards\"},{\"title\":\"Get latest staking operation\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/networks/{network_id}/addresses/{address_id}/staking_operations/{staking_operation_id}\",\"description\":\"Fetches the latest staking operation for the specified network and address given the staking operation ID.\\n\\nThis API can be used to poll the status of a staking operation and check if it has been successfully completed or not.\\n\\nA completed staking operation will have a status of `completed` which is indicative of the fact that all staking transactions have been successfully created and no more staking transactions will be generated.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/get-latest-staking-operation\"},{\"title\":\"Get staking context\",\"openapi\":\"POST /v1/stake/context\",\"description\":\"Fetches the staking context for an address given the network, asset, address and [custom options](/staking/staking-api/introduction/api-usage#staking-options).\\n\\nThis API offers a point-in-time snapshot of key staking details, such as current stakeable, unstakeable, and claimable balances, which reflect the address’s staking position at the time of the request.\\n\\nThis information can be used to gate staking actions on your platform or to provide users with precise details on their staking positions. For example, knowing the current unstakeable balance allows you to inform users of the maximum amount they can unstake at that moment.\\n\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/get-staking-context\"},{\"title\":\"Get validator by ID\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/networks/{network_id}/assets/{asset_id}/validators/{validator_id}\",\"description\":\"Get a validator by its ID for a given network and asset.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/get-validator-by-id\"},{\"title\":\"List validators\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/networks/{network_id}/assets/{asset_id}/validators\",\"description\":\"List validators belonging to the user for a given network and asset.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/staking/list-validators\"}]},{\"group\":\"Onramp/Offramp\",\"pages\":[{\"title\":\"Create buy quote\",\"openapi\":\"POST /v1/buy/quote\",\"description\":\"The Buy Quote API provides clients with a quote based on the asset the user would like to purchase, the network they want to receive it on, the fiat amount of the payment, the payment currency, the payment method, and country of the user.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/create-buy-quote\"},{\"title\":\"Create sell quote\",\"openapi\":\"POST /v1/sell/quote\",\"description\":\"The Sell Quote API provides clients with a quote based on the asset the user would like to sell, the network they plan to send it on, the crypto amount of the sale, the sale currency, the fiat deposit method, and country of the user.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/create-sell-quote\"},{\"title\":\"Create session token\",\"openapi\":\"POST /v1/token\",\"description\":\"Creates a single use token that can be used to initialize an Onramp or Offramp session.\\n This API should be called once for every new user session. The returned token will expire after 5 minutes.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/create-session-token\"},{\"title\":\"Get all offramp transactions\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/sell/transactions\",\"description\":\"Returns all offramp transactions created by your app in the provided time period.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-all-offramp-transactions\"},{\"title\":\"Get all onramp transactions\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/buy/transactions\",\"description\":\"Returns all onramp transactions created by your app in the provided time period.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-all-onramp-transactions\"},{\"title\":\"Get buy config\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/buy/config\",\"description\":\"The Buy Config API returns the list of countries supported by Coinbase Pay Onramp, and the payment methods available in each country. Clients should call this API periodically and cache the response.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-buy-config\"},{\"title\":\"Get buy options\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/buy/options\",\"description\":\"The Buy Options API provides clients with a way to discover the available options for buying Crypto with Coinbase Onramp. It returns the supported fiat currencies and available crypto assets that can be passed into the Buy Quote API. Clients should call this API periodically for each country they support and cache the response.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-buy-options\"},{\"title\":\"Get offramp transactions by ID\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/sell/user/{partnerUserRef}/transactions\",\"description\":\"When you initialize the Offramp Widget you can pass a `partnerUserRef` parameter to associate the transaction created during that session with the ID. You can then use that ID in this API to retrieve that transaction and any others that share the ID. The value of the `partnerUserRef` param can be any string you want e.g. the ID of the user in your app, a unique transaction ID, or a combination of values. Anything that is meaningful to your app.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-offramp-transactions-by-id\"},{\"title\":\"Get onramp transactions by ID\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/buy/user/{partnerUserRef}/transactions\",\"description\":\"When you initialize the Onramp Widget you can pass a `partnerUserRef` parameter to associate the transaction created during that session with the ID. You can then use that ID in this API to retrieve that transaction and any others that share the ID. The value of the `partnerUserRef` param can be any string you want e.g. the ID of the user in your app, a unique transaction ID, or a combination of values. Anything that is meaningful to your app.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-onramp-transactions-by-id\"},{\"title\":\"Get sell config\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/sell/config\",\"description\":\"The Sell Config API returns the list of countries supported by Coinbase Pay Offramp, and the fiat deposit methods available in each country. Clients should call this API periodically and cache the response.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-sell-config\"},{\"title\":\"Get sell options\",\"openapi\":\"GET /v1/sell/options\",\"description\":\"The Sell Options API provides clients with a way to discover the available options for selling Crypto with Coinbase Offramp. It returns the supported fiat deposit currencies and available crypto assets that can be passed into the Sell Quote API. Clients should call this API periodically for each country they support and cache the response.\",\"href\":\"/api-reference/rest-api/onramp-offramp/get-sell-options\"}]}]},{\"group\":\"JSON-RPC
